title: Contributing to phenoQC
text: Thank interest contributing!

title: Contributing to phenoQC
text: Fork repository GitHub Clone fork locally Create branch feature fix Make changes following tidyverse style guide Test devtools::check() Submit pull request

title: Contributing to phenoQC
text: Add function appropriate module file Follow pattern: data first arg, return augmented data tibble Use check_col_exists() input validation Add roxygen2 docs @examples Write tests tests/testthat/ Update NEWS.md

title: Contributing to phenoQC
text: Use GitHub issue tracker reproducible example.

title: Getting Started with phenoQC
text: phenoQC provides automated, reproducible quality control phenotypic data plant breeding field trials. vignette walks typical QC session bundled example_trial dataset: simulated 197-plot trial handful deliberate data quality issues baked (duplicate plot coordinates, missing values, extreme yield outlier, unreplicated genotype, spatial gradient plant height).

title: Getting Started with phenoQC
text: Notice rows 5 6 already show one injected issues: share row/col position (1, 5).
code: data(example_trial) dim(example_trial) #> [1] 197   8  head(example_trial) #>   row col rep block genotype yield plant_height days_to_flower #> 1   1   1   1     1   CHECK1  5.39         83.8             67 #> 2   1   2   1     1     G037  4.84         83.3             66 #> 3   1   3   1     1     G001  4.26         81.9             64 #> 4   1   4   1     1     G025  4.85         85.3             64 #> 5   1   5   1     1     G010  4.06         82.6             62 #> 6   1   5   1     1     G036  5.91         92.3             69

title: Getting Started with phenoQC
text: Rather typing trait names hand, detect_trait_cols() inspects numeric columns excludes common structural fields like row, col, rep, block:
code: trait_cols <- detect_trait_cols(example_trial) trait_cols #> [1] "yield"          "plant_height"   "days_to_flower"

title: Getting Started with phenoQC
text: phenoqc() main entry point. runs structure validation, missing data analysis, outlier detection (IQR + spatial default), spatial trend diagnostics one call: result phenoqc_result object print() method quick overview:
code: result <- phenoqc(example_trial, trait_cols = trait_cols) #> ── phenoQC: Phenotypic Data Quality Control ────────────────────────────── #> #> ── Step 1: Validating trial structure ── #> #> ! 3 issue(s) found in trial structure. #> #> ── Step 2: Analysing missing data ── #> #> ℹ 5 missing value(s) across 3 trait(s). #> #> ── Step 3: Detecting outliers ── #> #> ℹ 5 outlier(s) flagged. #> #> ── Step 4: Checking spatial trends ── #> #> ! 1 trait(s) show spatial trend. #> #> ── QC Complete ── #> #> ✔ Use `summary()` for an overview or `qc_report()` to generate an HTML #>   report. result #> #> ── phenoQC Result #> • Observations: 197 #> • Traits: yield, plant_height, days_to_flower #> • Outliers: 6 total flagged #> • Missing: 5 total #> • Spatial: 1 trait(s) with trend

title: Getting Started with phenoQC
text: summary() returns per-trait overview tibble: plant_height flagged spatial_trend = TRUE shows one outlier even though IQR-outlier count zero – ’s combined IQR + spatial flag work, covered depth vignette("spatial-analysis") article. full result also exposes intermediate table individually:
code: summary(result) #> # A tibble: 3 × 7 #>   trait          n_obs n_missing n_outliers spatial_trend mean_raw sd_raw #>   <chr>          <int>     <int>      <int> <lgl>            <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 yield            194         3          1 FALSE             5.02   1.26 #> 2 plant_height     195         2          1 TRUE             87.9    6.61 #> 3 days_to_flower   197         0          4 FALSE            64.7    2.42 result$validation #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   check                      status n_issues details #>   <chr>                      <chr>     <int> <glue> #> 1 Duplicate plot coordinates fail          2 2 duplicate position(s) detected #> 2 Missing plot positions     warn          4 4 position(s) missing from grid #> 3 Trait column types         pass          0 All trait columns are numeric #> 4 Replication balance        warn          1 1 genotype(s) with unequal replication  result$missing #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   trait          n_total n_missing pct_missing #>   <chr>            <int>     <int>       <dbl> #> 1 yield              197         3         1.5 #> 2 plant_height       197         2         1 #> 3 days_to_flower     197         0         0

title: Getting Started with phenoQC
text: step phenoqc() also available standalone function, useful interactive exploration building custom pipeline.

title: Getting Started with phenoQC
code: qc_check_duplicates(example_trial) #> # A tibble: 2 × 8 #>     row   col   rep block genotype yield plant_height days_to_flower #>   <int> <int> <int> <int> <chr>    <dbl>        <dbl>          <int> #> 1     1     5     1     1 G010      4.06         82.6             62 #> 2     1     5     1     1 G036      5.91         92.3             69

title: Getting Started with phenoQC
text: ’s injected extreme yield value (18.5 t/ha trial mean around 5 t/ha) – easily caught simple IQR rule.
code: yield_out <- qc_outliers_statistical(example_trial, "yield", method = "iqr") attr(yield_out, "outlier_summary") #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   trait method threshold n_total n_outliers pct_outliers #>   <chr> <chr>      <dbl>   <int>      <int>        <dbl> #> 1 yield iqr          1.5     194          1          0.5  yield_out[yield_out$yield_outlier, c("row", "col", "genotype", "yield", "yield_zscore")] #>    row col genotype yield yield_zscore #> 50   5  10     G009  18.5       10.731

title: Getting Started with phenoQC
text: plot flagged spatially , large positive residual relative field neighbours.
code: yield_spatial <- qc_outliers_spatial(example_trial, "yield") yield_spatial[   yield_spatial$yield_spatial_outlier,   c("row", "col", "genotype", "yield", "yield_spatial_residual") ] #>    row col genotype yield yield_spatial_residual #> 50   5  10     G009  18.5                    13.6

what: Plotting outliers on the field layout
title: Getting Started with phenoQC
text: qc_plot_outliers() draws field heatmap raw trait values overlays “X” marker plot flagged qc_flag_outliers().
code: qc_plot_outliers(qc_flag_outliers(example_trial, "yield"), "yield")

title: Getting Started with phenoQC
text: ’re happy QC run, qc_report() renders everything – structure checks, missing data plots, outlier summaries, field heatmaps, spatial diagnostics – single HTML report: Set open = TRUE (default interactive sessions) report open automatically browser.
code: qc_report(result, output_file = "example_trial_qc.html", open = FALSE) #> ℹ Rendering QC report to 'example_trial_qc.html'... #> ✔ Report saved to 'example_trial_qc.html'

title: Spatial Outlier Detection and Trend Diagnostics
text: Field trials laid grid, neighbouring plots rarely independent: soil fertility gradients, moisture, shading, management effects vary smoothly across field. purely statistical outlier method like IQR z-score treats every observation exchangeable ignores structure – can miss plot perfectly “normal” relative whole trial starkly different immediate neighbours (can also -flag values extreme overall expected given strong field trend). phenoQC offers : qc_outliers_statistical() – IQR, z-score, MAD outlier rules applied trait’s overall distribution. qc_outliers_spatial() – flags plots deviate mean k nearest field neighbours. vignette shows case example_trial two methods disagree, covers spatial trend autocorrelation diagnostics.

what: A case where spatial catches what IQR misses
title: Spatial Outlier Detection and Trend Diagnostics
text: example_trial$plant_height deliberate left--right spatial gradient baked (taller plants higher-numbered columns), top usual plot--plot noise. Run outlier methods : IQR finds nothing unusual – every plant height value falls within plausible range trial whole. spatial method flags one plot: column 8, field-wide gradient means plots quite tall (column position pushes plant height trial). height 79.3 cm unremarkable trial-wide, well immediate neighbours columns 7-9 showing – exactly kind local anomaly qc_outliers_spatial() designed catch global IQR rule, construction, see. Note reverse also happens yield: single extreme value (18.5 t/ha row 5, col 10) far outside trial’s range methods agree – spatial methods complement statistical ones, don’t replace . practice, qc_flag_outliers() (used internally phenoqc()) runs combines flags.
code: ph_iqr <- qc_outliers_statistical(example_trial, "plant_height", method = "iqr") ph_spatial <- qc_outliers_spatial(example_trial, "plant_height")  sum(ph_iqr$plant_height_outlier, na.rm = TRUE) #> [1] 0  sum(ph_spatial$plant_height_spatial_outlier, na.rm = TRUE) #> [1] 1 only_spatial <- which(ph_spatial$plant_height_spatial_outlier &   !ph_iqr$plant_height_outlier) example_trial[only_spatial, c("row", "col", "genotype", "plant_height")] #>    row col genotype plant_height #> 18   2   8     G031         79.3

title: Spatial Outlier Detection and Trend Diagnostics
text: qc_spatial_trend() fits 2D loess surface field coordinates reports much trait’s variance spatial trend explains: half raw variance plant_height explained position field (var_ratio = 0.521) – confirming gradient know design. Compare yield, designed spatial structure:
code: trend_result <- qc_spatial_trend(example_trial, "plant_height") attr(trend_result, "spatial_diagnostic") #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   trait        var_raw var_trend var_ratio has_trend interpretation #>   <chr>          <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> <lgl>     <chr> #> 1 plant_height    43.7      22.8     0.521 TRUE      Strong spatial trend detected attr(qc_spatial_trend(example_trial, "yield"), "spatial_diagnostic") #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   trait var_raw var_trend var_ratio has_trend interpretation #>   <chr>   <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> <lgl>     <chr> #> 1 yield    1.58     0.103    0.0651 FALSE     No significant spatial trend

what: Plotting raw values, trend, and residuals
title: Spatial Outlier Detection and Trend Diagnostics
text: qc_plot_spatial() facets raw field layout, fitted trend surface, residuals side side: raw trend panels look visibly similar (left--right colour gradient) genuine spatial trend present; residual panel remaining spatial anomalies – like row 18 – become easier spot visually.
code: qc_plot_spatial(trend_result, "plant_height")

what: Testing spatial autocorrelation with Moran’s I
title: Spatial Outlier Detection and Trend Diagnostics
text: Beyond loess-based trend test, qc_spatial_autocorrelation() computes Moran’s , formal statistic whether nearby plots similar expected spatial randomness. positive Moran’s well expected value null, p-value essentially 0, confirms significant spatial clustering plant_height – consistent trend result . Compare trait spatial structure: yield days_to_flower, observed Moran’s close expectation p-value large – evidence spatial autocorrelation, matching fact neither trait designed field trend.
code: qc_spatial_autocorrelation(example_trial, "plant_height") #> $statistic #> [1] 0.1311 #> #> $expected #> [1] -0.0052 #> #> $p_value #> [1] 0 #> #> $interpretation #> [1] "Significant positive spatial autocorrelation (clustering)" qc_spatial_autocorrelation(example_trial, "yield") #> $statistic #> [1] -0.0091 #> #> $expected #> [1] -0.0052 #> #> $p_value #> [1] 0.7219 #> #> $interpretation #> [1] "No significant spatial autocorrelation" qc_spatial_autocorrelation(example_trial, "days_to_flower") #> $statistic #> [1] -0.0051 #> #> $expected #> [1] -0.0051 #> #> $p_value #> [1] 0.9998 #> #> $interpretation #> [1] "No significant spatial autocorrelation"

title: Spatial Outlier Detection and Trend Diagnostics
text: Run qc_spatial_autocorrelation() per trait quick screening test. traits significant autocorrelation, follow qc_spatial_trend() quantify visualise trend, consider whether reflects real field variation modelled (e.g. spatial mixed model) rather treated noise. Always run qc_outliers_spatial() alongside statistical method – phenoqc() default via outlier_methods = c("iqr", "spatial") – local anomalies aren’t missed just look unremarkable whole-trial level.

what: Automated Phenotypic Data Quality Control for Plant Breeding Trials
title: Automated Phenotypic Data Quality Control for Plant Breeding Trials
text: phenoQC provides automated, reproducible quality control phenotypic data plant breeding field trials. detects spatial outliers, validates trial structure, diagnoses spatial trends, generates HTML QC reports — single function call.

title: Automated Phenotypic Data Quality Control for Plant Breeding Trials
text: phenoQC complements AllInOne-P — use AllInOne-P interactive data exploration, use phenoQC reproducible QC pipelines run every trial.

title: Automated Phenotypic Data Quality Control for Plant Breeding Trials
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("josh45-source/phenoQC")

title: Automated Phenotypic Data Quality Control for Plant Breeding Trials
code: library(phenoQC)  # Load example data data(example_trial)  # Run full QC with one function call result <- phenoqc(   example_trial,   trait_cols = c("yield", "plant_height", "days_to_flower") )  # View summary summary(result)  # Generate HTML report qc_report(result, "trial_qc_report.html")

title: Automated Phenotypic Data Quality Control for Plant Breeding Trials
code: # Validate trial structure qc_validate_structure(example_trial,   trait_cols = c("yield", "plant_height"))  # Detect statistical outliers flagged <- qc_outliers_statistical(example_trial, "yield", method = "iqr")  # Detect spatial outliers (catches what IQR misses) flagged <- qc_outliers_spatial(example_trial, "yield")  # Visualise outliers on field layout qc_plot_outliers(flagged, "yield")  # Check for spatial trends trended <- qc_spatial_trend(example_trial, "plant_height") qc_plot_spatial(trended, "plant_height")  # Impute missing values using spatial neighbors imputed <- qc_impute_spatial(example_trial, "yield")

title: Automated Phenotypic Data Quality Control for Plant Breeding Trials
code: library(brapiR2) library(phenoQC)  # Pull data from BreedBase con <- brapi_connection("https://my-breedbase.org") con <- brapi_login(con, "user", "pass") data <- brapi_study_data(con, "my_study")  # QC it result <- phenoqc(data, trait_cols = c("yield", "plant_height")) qc_report(result, "breedbase_qc.html")  # Use cleaned data for analysis clean <- result$cleaned_data

title: Example Field Trial Dataset — example_trial
text: simulated field trial dataset 200 observations testing phenoQC functions. trial 50 genotypes 4 reps laid 10-column x 20-row grid. Includes deliberate quality issues: duplicate plot coordinates, missing values, non-numeric entry yield, unreplicated genotype.

title: Example Field Trial Dataset — example_trial
code: example_trial

title: Example Field Trial Dataset — example_trial
text: data frame 200 rows 8 columns: row Integer. Row position field (1-20). col Integer. Column position field (1-10). rep Integer. Replicate number (1-4). block Integer. Incomplete block within rep (1-8). genotype Character. Genotype identifier (G001-G050 plus checks). yield Numeric. Grain yield t/ha. Contains outliers missing values. plant_height Numeric. Plant height cm. Contains spatial trend. days_to_flower Numeric. Days planting 50% flowering.

title: Example Field Trial Dataset — example_trial
text: Simulated data package testing.

title: Example Field Trial Dataset — example_trial
text: Known quality issues embedded dataset: 2 duplicate plot coordinates (row+col) 3 missing values yield 2 missing values plant_height 1 extreme yield outlier (value > 15 t/ha) 1 genotype 1 rep instead 4 Spatial gradient plant_height (increases left right)

what: phenoQC: Automated Phenotypic Data Quality Control for Plant Breeding Trials — phenoQC-package
title: phenoQC: Automated Phenotypic Data Quality Control for Plant Breeding Trials — phenoQC-package
text: Provides automated, reproducible quality control phenotypic data plant breeding field trials. Includes trial structure validation (duplicate plots, missing entries, replication balance), statistical spatial outlier detection, spatial trend diagnostics, missing data analysis spatial imputation, automated HTML QC report generation. functions pipe-friendly non-interactive, designed scripting batch processing. Integrates seamlessly 'brapiR2' end--end breeding data pipelines.

title: phenoQC: Automated Phenotypic Data Quality Control for Plant Breeding Trials — phenoQC-package
text: Maintainer: Joash Joshua Ayo joash.ayo@example.com (ORCID)

what: Run Complete Phenotypic Quality Control — phenoqc
title: Run Complete Phenotypic Quality Control — phenoqc
text: main entry point phenoQC. Runs full QC pipeline trial data frame: structure validation, missing data summary, outlier detection, spatial trend diagnostics.

title: Run Complete Phenotypic Quality Control — phenoqc
code: phenoqc(   data,   trait_cols,   row_col = "row",   col_col = "col",   gen_col = "genotype",   rep_col = "rep",   control_names = NULL,   outlier_methods = c("iqr", "spatial"),   iqr_threshold = 1.5,   spatial_k = 8L,   check_spatial_trend = TRUE,   impute_missing = FALSE )

title: Run Complete Phenotypic Quality Control — phenoqc
text: data data frame trial data. trait_cols Character vector. Names numeric trait columns QC. row_col Character. Row position column. Default "row". col_col Character. Column position column. Default "col". gen_col Character. Genotype column. Default "genotype". rep_col Character. Replicate column. Default "rep". control_names Character vector NULL. Known check/control names. outlier_methods Character vector. Outlier methods apply. Default c("iqr", "spatial"). iqr_threshold Numeric. IQR multiplier outlier detection. Default 1.5. spatial_k Integer. Neighbors spatial outlier detection. Default 8. check_spatial_trend Logical. Whether run spatial trend analysis. Default TRUE. impute_missing Logical. Whether impute missing values using spatial neighbors. Default FALSE.

title: Run Complete Phenotypic Quality Control — phenoqc
text: S3 object class "phenoqc_result" containing: data original data QC flag columns added. validation Trial structure validation summary. outliers Outlier summary per trait. missing Missing data summary. spatial Spatial trend diagnostics (run). cleaned_data Data outliers set NA. summary One-line-per-trait overview. params Parameters used QC run.

title: Run Complete Phenotypic Quality Control — phenoqc

what: Check for Duplicate Plot Coordinates — qc_check_duplicates
title: Check for Duplicate Plot Coordinates — qc_check_duplicates
text: Detects rows identical (row, col) positions. multi-environment multi-replicate trials physical grid normally reused every environment/rep, duplicates checked within groups rather across whole dataset.

title: Check for Duplicate Plot Coordinates — qc_check_duplicates
code: qc_check_duplicates(data, row_col = "row", col_col = "col", group_cols = NULL)

title: Check for Duplicate Plot Coordinates — qc_check_duplicates
text: data data frame. row_col Character. Name row column. col_col Character. Name column column. group_cols Character vector NULL. Columns define separate trials/blocks (e.g. c("env", "rep")) within (row, col) must unique. NULL (default), auto-detects env, rep, location, site, trial, study present data (case insensitive) uses grouping columns. Pass character(0) force global (ungrouped) check.

title: Check for Duplicate Plot Coordinates — qc_check_duplicates
text: tibble duplicated positions columns original data.

title: Summarise Missing Data — qc_missing_summary
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(example_trial) qc_missing_summary(example_trial, c("yield", "plant_height", "days_to_flower")) } # }

title: Detect Outliers Within Groups — qc_outliers_by_group
text: Runs IQR outlier detection within level grouping variable (e.g. within genotype replicate).

title: Detect Outliers Within Groups — qc_outliers_by_group
code: qc_outliers_by_group(data, trait_cols, group_col, threshold = 1.5)

title: Detect Outliers Within Groups — qc_outliers_by_group
text: data data frame. trait_cols Character vector. Names numeric trait columns. group_col Character. Name grouping column. threshold Numeric. IQR multiplier. Default 1.5.

title: Detect Outliers Within Groups — qc_outliers_by_group
text: input data frame {trait}_group_outlier columns.

title: Detect Spatial Outliers — qc_outliers_spatial
text: Flags observations outliers relative spatial neighbors field layout. Uses k-nearest-neighbor local means compute residuals, flags observations extreme residuals.

title: Detect Spatial Outliers — qc_outliers_spatial
code: qc_outliers_spatial(   data,   trait_cols,   row_col = "row",   col_col = "col",   k = 8L,   threshold = 3 )

title: Detect Spatial Outliers — qc_outliers_spatial
text: data data frame. trait_cols Character vector. Names numeric trait columns. row_col Character. Name row position column. Default "row". col_col Character. Name column position column. Default "col". k Integer. Number nearest neighbors use. Default 8. threshold Numeric. MAD multiplier flagging. Default 3.

title: Detect Spatial Outliers — qc_outliers_spatial
text: input data frame {trait}_spatial_outlier (logical) {trait}_spatial_residual (numeric) columns added.

title: Detect Statistical Outliers — qc_outliers_statistical
text: Flags observations outliers using IQR, z-score, MAD methods.

title: Detect Statistical Outliers — qc_outliers_statistical
code: qc_outliers_statistical(data, trait_cols, method = "iqr", threshold = NULL)

title: Detect Statistical Outliers — qc_outliers_statistical
text: data data frame. trait_cols Character vector. Names numeric trait columns. method Character. One "iqr", "zscore", "mad". threshold Numeric. Multiplier outlier boundary. Default 1.5 IQR, 3 z-score/MAD.

title: Detect Statistical Outliers — qc_outliers_statistical
text: input data frame additional columns trait: {trait}_outlier (logical) {trait}_zscore (numeric). "outlier_summary" attribute attached per-trait counts.

title: Detect Statistical Outliers — qc_outliers_statistical
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(example_trial) result <- qc_outliers_statistical(example_trial, c("yield", "plant_height")) attr(result, "outlier_summary") } # }

title: Plot Outliers on Field Layout — qc_plot_outliers
text: Creates field heatmap showing trait values outliers highlighted.

title: Plot Outliers on Field Layout — qc_plot_outliers
code: qc_plot_outliers(data, trait, row_col = "row", col_col = "col")

title: Plot Outliers on Field Layout — qc_plot_outliers
text: data data frame (outlier columns qc_flag_outliers qc_outliers_statistical). trait Character. Name trait column plot. row_col Character. Row position column. Default "row". col_col Character. Column position column. Default "col".

title: Plot Outliers on Field Layout — qc_plot_outliers
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot Spatial Field Heatmap — qc_plot_spatial
text: Creates field layout heatmap showing raw values, fitted trend, residuals.

title: Plot Spatial Field Heatmap — qc_plot_spatial

title: Plot Spatial Field Heatmap — qc_plot_spatial
text: data data frame (trend/residual columns qc_spatial_trend()). trait Character. Base trait name. row_col Character. Row position column. Default "row". col_col Character. Column position column. Default "col". Character vector. display: "raw", "trend", /"residual". Default shows three.

title: Plot Spatial Field Heatmap — qc_plot_spatial
text: ggplot object (faceted multiple values).

what: Generate an HTML Quality Control Report — qc_report
title: Generate an HTML Quality Control Report — qc_report
text: Takes phenoqc() result renders HTML report validation results, missing data visualization, outlier summaries, field heatmaps, spatial trend diagnostics.

title: Generate an HTML Quality Control Report — qc_report
code: qc_report(   result,   output_file = "qc_report.html",   title = "Phenotypic QC Report",   open = interactive() )

title: Generate an HTML Quality Control Report — qc_report
text: result phenoqc_result object phenoqc(). output_file Character. Path output HTML file. Default "qc_report.html". title Character. Report title. Default "Phenotypic QC Report". open Logical. Open report browser rendering? Default TRUE interactive sessions.

title: Generate an HTML Quality Control Report — qc_report
text: path generated report (invisibly).

title: Generate an HTML Quality Control Report — qc_report
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(example_trial) result <- phenoqc(example_trial,   trait_cols = c("yield", "plant_height", "days_to_flower") ) qc_report(result, "my_trial_qc.html") } # }

title: Test Spatial Autocorrelation (Moran's I) — qc_spatial_autocorrelation
text: Calculates Moran's statistic test whether trait values spatially autocorrelated field layout.

title: Test Spatial Autocorrelation (Moran's I) — qc_spatial_autocorrelation
code: qc_spatial_autocorrelation(data, trait, row_col = "row", col_col = "col")

title: Test Spatial Autocorrelation (Moran's I) — qc_spatial_autocorrelation
text: data data frame. trait Character. Name trait column. row_col Character. Row position column. Default "row". col_col Character. Column position column. Default "col".

title: Test Spatial Autocorrelation (Moran's I) — qc_spatial_autocorrelation
text: list components: statistic (Moran's value), expected (expected null), p_value, interpretation.

what: Detect and Extract Spatial Trends — qc_spatial_trend
title: Detect and Extract Spatial Trends — qc_spatial_trend
text: Fits 2D loess surface trait using field coordinates returns trend, residuals, diagnostic indicating whether significant spatial trend exists.

title: Detect and Extract Spatial Trends — qc_spatial_trend
code: qc_spatial_trend(data, trait, row_col = "row", col_col = "col", span = 0.5)

title: Detect and Extract Spatial Trends — qc_spatial_trend
text: data data frame. trait Character. Name trait column. row_col Character. Row position column. Default "row". col_col Character. Column position column. Default "col". span Numeric. Smoothing parameter loess. Default 0.5.

title: Detect and Extract Spatial Trends — qc_spatial_trend
text: input data frame {trait}_trend {trait}_residual columns added. attribute "spatial_diagnostic" contains tibble trend strength metrics.

Default "rep". gen_col Character. Name genotype column. Default "genotype". trait_cols Character vector. Names trait (numeric) columns check.

title: Validate Trial Structure — qc_validate_structure
text: tibble columns: check, status (pass/warn/fail), n_issues, details.

text: object phenoqc_result object. ... Ignored.

title: Summarise a phenoQC Result — summary.phenoqc_result
text: trait summary tibble (printed returned invisibly).

title: phenoQC (development version)
text: Validation module: qc_validate_structure(), qc_check_duplicates(), qc_check_missing_plots(), qc_check_trait_types(), qc_check_replication(), qc_check_controls(). Outlier detection: qc_outliers_statistical() (IQR, z-score, MAD), qc_outliers_spatial() (k-NN residuals), qc_outliers_by_group(), qc_flag_outliers() (combined), qc_plot_outliers(). Spatial diagnostics: qc_spatial_trend() (loess surface), qc_plot_spatial(), qc_spatial_autocorrelation() (Moran’s ). Missing data: qc_missing_summary(), qc_missing_plot(), qc_impute_spatial() (k-NN / median neighbor imputation). Main wrapper: phenoqc() runs full QC pipeline returns phenoqc_result object data, validation, outlier, missing, spatial, cleaned data components. Reporting: qc_report() generates HTML QC report. Utilities: detect_trait_cols() auto-detection numeric traits. Built-example_trial dataset deliberate QC issues.
